## HNSW Small-Dataset Note

> HNSW is an approximate O(log N) graph index, but this benchmark dataset is too small to expose the approximation trade-off. With N=20, the graph has so few nodes that `faiss_hnsw_l2` is expected to match the exact FAISS L2 ranking and MRR. HNSW recall differences become meaningful at thousands of vectors and above.


## Operation Count Scaling

> **Operation count** is the cross-engine scaling KPI.
> Exact classical engines perform N comparisons per query (linear scan).
> HNSW performs an approximate graph traversal with O(log N) expected query cost.
> Grover's algorithm uses floor(π√N/4) oracle calls per query.
> This comparison is hardware-independent - it measures algorithmic efficiency,
> not wall-clock time.

| Engine               | Dataset Size (N) | Operations per Query | Complexity Class     |
| -------------------- | ---------------- | -------------------- | -------------------- |
| `brute_force_cosine` | 20               | 20                   | O(N)                 |
| `faiss_flat_l2`      | 20               | 20                   | O(N)                 |
| `faiss_hnsw_l2`      | 20               | 5                    | O(log N) approximate |
| `qiskit_grover`      | 20               | 4                    | O(√N)                |
| `qiskit_swap_test`   | 20               | 20                   | O(N)                 |

## Quantum: Shots vs. Quality

> Shots = number of quantum circuit measurements per query.
> On real hardware, fewer shots = lower cost. This table shows the minimum shots
> needed to reach acceptable MRR.

| Shots | MRR   | Runs |
| ----- | ----- | ---- |
| 512   | 0.592 | 80   |
| 1024  | 0.607 | 80   |

## Quantum Circuit Complexity

> Circuit metrics are hardware-agnostic cost proxies.
> **Circuit depth** = number of sequential gate layers; deeper circuits are more susceptible to decoherence on real hardware.
> **Qubits** = number of qubits required; more qubits = harder to allocate on near-term devices.
> For the mock engine these are theoretical estimates based on amplitude encoding.
> For the swap-test engine these are extracted from the actual compiled Qiskit circuit.

| Engine             | Dimension | Dataset Size | Circuit Depth | Qubits |
| ------------------ | --------- | ------------ | ------------- | ------ |
| `qiskit_grover`    | 64        | 20           | 50            | 5      |
| `qiskit_grover`    | 128       | 20           | 50            | 5      |
| `qiskit_swap_test` | 64        | 20           | 9             | 13     |
| `qiskit_swap_test` | 128       | 20           | 10            | 15     |
